revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:日光二荒山神社, en:Futarasan shrine); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/55994429 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) http://www.futarasan.jp/ を見ていただくとわかるように、「日光二荒山神社」が静止意みたいなので、記事を移動しようと思います。異存はありませんか? [[利用者:秀の介|秀の介]] 2003年12月24日 (水) 01:10 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1202178841 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|shinto=yes}} {{WikiProject Historic sites|importance=mid}} }} == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Futarasan jinja]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=758598944 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf to http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 12:13, 6 January 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Futarasan jinja]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=804452216 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20070929000758/http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/futarasan.html to http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/futarasan.html When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 02:43, 9 October 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Phallic mountain?? == I am baffled by the text in the [[Futarasan_jinja#History_of_the_shrine|#History_of_the_shrine]] section, purportedly from the 1993 book ''Cambridge History of Japan'', page 524, stating that:
The mountain ... has the shape of the phallic stone rods found in pre-agricultural Jōmon sites.
How is the shape of the mountain even remotely phallic? None of the photographs in the article show this, nor does any map that I've seen. I don't have access to the ''Cambridge History of Japan'' and cannot confirm if the given source actually makes this claim, nor how they explain this. Interestingly, I find no mention of phallic-ness in any of the monolingual Japanese sources I've just looked into, including: * JA WP article for Mt. Nantai at [[:ja:男体山]] * JA WP article for Futarasan Jinja at [[:ja:二荒山神社]] * Kotobank page for https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B7%E4%BD%93%E5%B1%B1-108859 :: Includes content from ''[[Encyclopedia Nipponica]]'', ''[[Encyclopedia Brittanica]]'' (Japanese edition), [[Japanese_encyclopedias#Sekai_Dai-Hyakka_Jiten|''Mypedia'']], Shogakukan's ''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', ''Digital [[Daijisen]]'', ''Dictionary of Japanese Heritage Sites'', ''Dictionary of Japanese Tourism Resources'', ''[[Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten]]''. Where these sources describe the name of the mountain at all, they explain that the "man body" name arose from association between this mountain and another nearby mountain as a pair, with one called {{nihongo_krt||女体山|Nyotai-san|literally "woman body mountain"}} and the other called {{nihongo_krt||男体山|Nantai-san|literally "man body mountain"}}. There's nothing about genitalia or phallicness. Even should the ''Cambridge History of Japan'' clearly state a claim that the mountain has a phallic shape, we need some further explanation -- visually, objectively, the mountain is not phallic in shape at all. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:51, 21 January 2022 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/55994429 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) http://www.futarasan.jp/ を見ていただくとわかるように、「日光二荒山神社」が静止意みたいなので、記事を移動しようと思います。異存はありませんか? [[User:秀の介|秀の介]] 2003年12月24日 (水) 01:10 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1202178841 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|shinto=yes}} {{WikiProject Historic sites|importance=mid}} }} == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Futarasan jinja]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=758598944 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf to http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 12:13, 6 January 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Futarasan jinja]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=804452216 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20070929000758/http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/futarasan.html to http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/futarasan.html When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 02:43, 9 October 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Phallic mountain?? == I am baffled by the text in the [[Futarasan_jinja#History_of_the_shrine|#History_of_the_shrine]] section, purportedly from the 1993 book ''Cambridge History of Japan'', page 524, stating that:
The mountain ... has the shape of the phallic stone rods found in pre-agricultural Jōmon sites.
How is the shape of the mountain even remotely phallic? None of the photographs in the article show this, nor does any map that I've seen. I don't have access to the ''Cambridge History of Japan'' and cannot confirm if the given source actually makes this claim, nor how they explain this. Interestingly, I find no mention of phallic-ness in any of the monolingual Japanese sources I've just looked into, including: * JA WP article for Mt. Nantai at [[:ja:男体山]] * JA WP article for Futarasan Jinja at [[:ja:二荒山神社]] * Kotobank page for https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B7%E4%BD%93%E5%B1%B1-108859 :: Includes content from ''[[Encyclopedia Nipponica]]'', ''[[Encyclopedia Brittanica]]'' (Japanese edition), [[Japanese_encyclopedias#Sekai_Dai-Hyakka_Jiten|''Mypedia'']], Shogakukan's ''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', ''Digital [[Daijisen]]'', ''Dictionary of Japanese Heritage Sites'', ''Dictionary of Japanese Tourism Resources'', ''[[Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten]]''. Where these sources describe the name of the mountain at all, they explain that the "man body" name arose from association between this mountain and another nearby mountain as a pair, with one called {{nihongo_krt||女体山|Nyotai-san|literally "woman body mountain"}} and the other called {{nihongo_krt||男体山|Nantai-san|literally "man body mountain"}}. There's nothing about genitalia or phallicness. Even should the ''Cambridge History of Japan'' clearly state a claim that the mountain has a phallic shape, we need some further explanation -- visually, objectively, the mountain is not phallic in shape at all. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:51, 21 January 2022 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Futarasan_shrine here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/55994429 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) http://www.futarasan.jp/ を見ていただくとわかるように、「日光二荒山神社」が静止意みたいなので、記事を移動しようと思います。異存はありませんか? [[User:秀の介|秀の介]] 2003年12月24日 (水) 01:10 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1202178841 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|shinto=yes}} {{WikiProject Historic sites|importance=mid}} }} == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Futarasan jinja]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=758598944 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf to http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 12:13, 6 January 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Futarasan jinja]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=804452216 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20070929000758/http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/futarasan.html to http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/futarasan.html When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 02:43, 9 October 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Phallic mountain?? == I am baffled by the text in the [[Futarasan_jinja#History_of_the_shrine|#History_of_the_shrine]] section, purportedly from the 1993 book ''Cambridge History of Japan'', page 524, stating that:
The mountain ... has the shape of the phallic stone rods found in pre-agricultural Jōmon sites.
How is the shape of the mountain even remotely phallic? None of the photographs in the article show this, nor does any map that I've seen. I don't have access to the ''Cambridge History of Japan'' and cannot confirm if the given source actually makes this claim, nor how they explain this. Interestingly, I find no mention of phallic-ness in any of the monolingual Japanese sources I've just looked into, including: * JA WP article for Mt. Nantai at [[:ja:男体山]] * JA WP article for Futarasan Jinja at [[:ja:二荒山神社]] * Kotobank page for https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%94%B7%E4%BD%93%E5%B1%B1-108859 :: Includes content from ''[[Encyclopedia Nipponica]]'', ''[[Encyclopedia Brittanica]]'' (Japanese edition), [[Japanese_encyclopedias#Sekai_Dai-Hyakka_Jiten|''Mypedia'']], Shogakukan's ''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', ''Digital [[Daijisen]]'', ''Dictionary of Japanese Heritage Sites'', ''Dictionary of Japanese Tourism Resources'', ''[[Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten]]''. Where these sources describe the name of the mountain at all, they explain that the "man body" name arose from association between this mountain and another nearby mountain as a pair, with one called {{nihongo_krt||女体山|Nyotai-san|literally "woman body mountain"}} and the other called {{nihongo_krt||男体山|Nantai-san|literally "man body mountain"}}. There's nothing about genitalia or phallicness. Even should the ''Cambridge History of Japan'' clearly state a claim that the mountain has a phallic shape, we need some further explanation -- visually, objectively, the mountain is not phallic in shape at all. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 19:51, 21 January 2022 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
